Setting
Urban
3 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 3/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 4/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 4/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
Data not available — PBJ submission issue.
★★★★★ 1/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 1/5 Data not available — PBJ submission issue.
Short-Stay Quality Measures — Data not available — PBJ submission issue.

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 4.06 3.82 4.11 ≥ 3.48
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 0.54 0.67 0.55 ≥ 0.55 Below floor
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 1.01 0.85 1.02 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 2.51 2.30 2.54 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 1.55 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.05 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
3.63
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
0.38
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
3.68
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Staff turnover

Total nursing staff turnover
12.3%
annual
RN turnover
42.9%
annual
Administrators departed
0
last 12 months

Resident acuity

Nursing Case-Mix Index
1.35
higher acuity than avg
Case-Mix Index Ratio
0.98
facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2025-11-21 11 11 0 44 1 44
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2024-10-18 16 16 0 72 1 72
Total weighted health score
51.0
lower is better

Deficiencies (50)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

6 A/B/C No actual harm — minor
44 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. E 2025-11-21 2025-11-25
0583 Keep residents' personal and medical records private and confidential. D 2025-11-21 2025-11-25
0645 PASARR screening for Mental disorders or Intellectual Disabilities D 2025-11-21 2025-11-25
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2025-11-21 2025-11-25
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. E 2025-11-21 2025-12-01
0698 Provide safe, appropriate dialysis care/services for a resident who requires such services. D 2025-11-21 2025-11-25
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. D 2025-11-21 2025-11-25
0814 Dispose of garbage and refuse properly. D 2025-11-21 2025-12-01
0849 Arrange for the provision of hospice services or assist the resident in transferring to a facility that will arrange for the provision of hospice services. D 2025-11-21 2025-11-25
0911 Ensure resident rooms hold no more than 4 residents; for new construction after November 28, 2016, rooms hold no more than 2 residents. B 2025-11-21 2025-12-01
0912 Provide rooms that are at least 80 square feet per resident in multiple rooms and 100 square feet for single resident rooms. B 2025-11-21 2025-12-01
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. D 2024-10-18 2024-11-04
0558 Reasonably accommodate the needs and preferences of each resident. D 2024-10-18 2024-11-04
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2024-10-18 2024-11-04
0658 Ensure services provided by the nursing facility meet professional standards of quality. D 2024-10-18 2024-11-04
0676 Ensure residents do not lose the ability to perform activities of daily living unless there is a medical reason. D 2024-10-18 2024-11-04
0686 Provide appropriate pressure ulcer care and prevent new ulcers from developing. D 2024-10-18 2024-11-05
0693 Ensure that feeding tubes are not used unless there is a medical reason and the resident agrees; and provide appropriate care for a resident with a feeding tube. D 2024-10-18 2024-11-04
0699 Provide care or services that was trauma informed and/or culturally competent. D 2024-10-18 2024-11-08
0726 Ensure that nurses and nurse aides have the appropriate competencies to care for every resident in a way that maximizes each resident's well being. D 2024-10-18 2024-10-24
0758 Implement gradual dose reductions(GDR) and non-pharmacological interventions, unless contraindicated, prior to initiating or instead of continuing psychotropic medication; and PRN orders for psychotropic medications are only used when the medication is necessary and PRN use is limited. D 2024-10-18 2024-11-04
0806 Ensure each resident receives and the facility provides food that accommodates resident allergies, intolerances, and preferences, as well as appealing options. D 2024-10-18 2024-11-04
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. E 2024-10-18 2024-10-30
0814 Dispose of garbage and refuse properly. E 2024-10-18 2024-11-04
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. E 2024-10-18 2024-11-04
0883 Develop and implement policies and procedures for flu and pneumonia vaccinations. D 2024-10-18 2024-11-04
0912 Provide rooms that are at least 80 square feet per resident in multiple rooms and 100 square feet for single resident rooms. B 2024-10-18 2024-11-11
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. D 2023-11-02 2023-11-27
0558 Reasonably accommodate the needs and preferences of each resident. D 2023-11-02 2023-11-27
0568 Properly hold, secure, and manage each resident's personal money which is deposited with the nursing home. D 2023-11-02 2023-11-27
0577 Allow residents to easily view the nursing home's survey results and communicate with advocate agencies. B 2023-11-02 2023-11-27
0578 Honor the resident's right to request, refuse, and/or discontinue treatment, to participate in or refuse to participate in experimental research, and to formulate an advance directive. D 2023-11-02 2023-11-27
0623 Provide timely notification to the resident, and if applicable to the resident representative and ombudsman, before transfer or discharge, including appeal rights. D 2023-11-02 2023-11-27
0655 Create and put into place a plan for meeting the resident's most immediate needs within 48 hours of being admitted D 2023-11-02 2023-11-27
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2023-11-02 2023-11-27
0657 Develop the complete care plan within 7 days of the comprehensive assessment; and prepared, reviewed, and revised by a team of health professionals. E 2023-11-02 2023-11-27
0679 Provide activities to meet all resident's needs. D 2023-11-02 2023-11-27
0686 Provide appropriate pressure ulcer care and prevent new ulcers from developing. D 2023-11-02 2023-11-27
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2023-11-02 2023-11-27
0692 Provide enough food/fluids to maintain a resident's health. E 2023-11-02 2023-11-27
0693 Ensure that feeding tubes are not used unless there is a medical reason and the resident agrees; and provide appropriate care for a resident with a feeding tube. D 2023-11-02 2023-11-27
0759 Ensure medication error rates are not 5 percent or greater. E 2023-11-02 2023-11-27
0760 Ensure that residents are free from significant medication errors. E 2023-11-02 2023-11-27
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. E 2023-11-02 2023-11-27
0803 Ensure menus must meet the nutritional needs of residents, be prepared in advance, be followed, be updated, be reviewed by dietician, and meet the needs of the resident. E 2023-11-02 2023-11-27
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. E 2023-11-02 2023-11-27
0849 Arrange for the provision of hospice services or assist the resident in transferring to a facility that will arrange for the provision of hospice services. D 2023-11-02 2023-11-27
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. D 2023-11-02 2023-11-27
0911 Ensure resident rooms hold no more than 4 residents; for new construction after November 28, 2016, rooms hold no more than 2 residents. B 2023-11-02 2023-11-27
0912 Provide rooms that are at least 80 square feet per resident in multiple rooms and 100 square feet for single resident rooms. B 2023-11-02 2023-11-27
